steps.title: Identify what the sentence needs
steps.content: The sentence is: "The 1958 poem 'The Ghost's Leavetaking' by American author Sylvia Plath ___ the first use of the word 'dreamscape.'" The subject is "The poem" (singular). There is no other verb — the blank must be the main, finite verb of the sentence.

steps.title: Choose the finite verb
steps.content: Rule: a main clause requires a finite verb (one that is conjugated and can stand alone as the predicate). Non-finite forms — participles, gerunds, infinitives — cannot serve as the main verb. A. "having contained" — past participle phrase (non-finite). Fails: "The poem having contained" leaves the sentence with no main verb; this form starts a participial modifier, not a predicate. B. "to contain" — infinitive (non-finite). Fails: "The poem to contain" is not a complete sentence. C. "containing" — present participle (non-finite). Fails: "The poem containing" is a fragment without a main verb. D. "contains" — third-person singular present tense (finite). Matches singular subject "The poem" and acts as the main predicate. Correct. That matches choice D — D is correct.
